title: Commentary on Policy/Limits Compliance: March 31, 2026
content:
BOPTI remains largely compliant with board-approved policy limits and targets for asset quality, capital solvency, interest rate risk, and liquidity. While several growth, efficiency, and funding metrics fall below best-practice ranges or exceed cost thresholds, the institution continues to maintain a sound overall financial position.

Asset Quality
Asset quality remains generally satisfactory. Classified assets to net worth of 0.03% remain well within policy limits (target <40%), while delinquent loans to total loans of 0.00% and net charge-offs to average loans of 0.00% both remain below their respective thresholds.

Capital & Solvency
Capital remains a key strength, with the solvency evaluation ratio at 1.10 (or 110%) significantly exceeding its benchmark target of >=7%. The net worth ratio stands at 0.10 (or 10%), which meets the minimum policy requirement of >=10%, and net worth growth of 0.07 (or 7%) exceeds the annualized YoY target of >=5%.

Earnings & Efficiency
Profitability metrics remain mixed, with some targets missed while others are met. Net margin at 4.00% remains within its guideline range of >=1.0%, and net interest margin at 3.00% meets the minimum target threshold. However, return on average assets (ROAA) stands at only 1.00%, which falls below the policy benchmark of >=8.0%. Additionally, operating expenses to total assets are reported as a raw figure that does not meet its <=35.00% limit.

Liquidity & Mix
Funding and liquidity metrics remain weak relative to preferred ranges. Regular shares to total funding stands at 25%, which is significantly below the target of >=70%. However, cash and short-term investments to total assets (8%) remains within its range benchmark of 8%-15%.

Interest Rate & Funding
Yield on average loans sits at 6.00%, comfortably above the minimum yield target of 4.00%. Cost of funds is reported as a raw figure that falls below the <=1.50% limit, indicating strong funding costs relative to assets.

Closing: The institution's capital and asset quality remain robust, providing a substantial buffer against potential losses despite weak growth in membership and efficiency metrics falling short of targets.
